perturbation.original_text: 6.3.2 unless within 14 days after the date of delivery the Owner delivers to NCC a counter-notice signed by a duly authorised officer of the Owner stating that no such failure has occurred or that any such failure has been rectified then NCC will release the Material to the Licensee upon receipt of the release fee stated in Schedule 4.
perturbation.changed_text: 6.3.2 unless within 30 days after the date of delivery the Owner delivers to NCC a counter-notice signed by a duly authorised officer of the Owner stating that no such failure has occurred or that any such failure has been rectified then NCC will release the Material to the Licensee upon receipt of the release fee stated in Schedule 4.
perturbation.explanation: The modification of clause 6.3.2, which extends the deadline for the Owner to deliver a counter-notice from 14 to 30 days, introduces a conflict with the overarching framework. Other sections of the agreement still rely on a shorter timeframe for dispute resolution and the release of materials, thus extending one deadline makes the process ambiguous.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6.4 Where there is any dispute as to the occurrence of any of the events or circumstances set out in Clause 6.1.1 to 6.1.4, 6.2 or 6.3 or the fulfilment of any obligations detailed therein, such dispute will be referred at the request of either the Owner or the Licensee to the Managing Director for the time being of NCC for the appointment of an expert who shall give a decision on the matter within 14 days of the date of referral or as soon as practicable thereafter. The expert's decision shall be final and binding as between the Owner and the Licensee except in the case of manifest error.
perturbation.justification: The original text specified a 14-day timeframe in clause 6.3.2 for the Owner to deliver a counter-notice. The changed text extends this timeframe to 30 days. The contradicted text in clause 6.4 refers to disputes related to clause 6.3 and specifies that an expert's decision should be given within 14 days of referral. By extending the timeframe in 6.3.2 to 30 days, the changed text introduces a contradiction, as the dispute resolution process in 6.4 still operates on a 14-day timeframe, creating an inconsistency regarding the time allowed for dispute resolution related to 6.3. This constitutes a direct contradiction, as per the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' definition, because different sections now provide conflicting deadlines.

perturbation.original_text: 11.1 NCC may terminate this Agreement after failure by the Owner or the Licensee to comply with a 30 day written notice from NCC to pay any outstanding fee. If the failure to pay is on the part of the Owner the Licensee shall be given the option of paying such fee itself. Such amount will be recoverable by the Licensee direct from the Owner.
perturbation.changed_text: 11.1 NCC may terminate this Agreement after failure by the Owner or the Licensee to comply with a 60 day written notice from NCC to pay any outstanding fee. If the failure to pay is on the part of the Owner the Licensee shall be given the option of paying such fee itself. Such amount will be recoverable by the Licensee direct from the Owner.
perturbation.explanation: By altering the notice period for payment failure from 30 to 60 days in clause 11.1, a contradiction arises in the enforcement of termination rights. This change introduces a timing conflict because the rest of the document is based on the understanding, and is using the period of 30 days as the term for the notice period. This contradiction can lead to confusion about the valid notice period, thus any action based on 30 days will be invalid, causing uncertainty.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 11.2 NCC may terminate this Agreement by giving 60 days written notice to the Owner and the Licensee. In that event the Owner and the Licensee shall appoint a mutually acceptable new custodian on terms similar to those contained in this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text alters the notice period for termination due to payment failure from 30 days to 60 days in clause 11.1. This directly contradicts clause 11.2, which specifies a 60-day notice period for termination by NCC under different circumstances. The inconsistency in notice periods creates confusion and potential legal challenges regarding which notice period applies in different termination scenarios, leading to an in-text contradiction as per the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.
